status: EXPLORATORY — not pre-registered. Discovered post-hoc after DepMap artifact.

depmap.interpretation: Essentiality is defined by magnitude; SD difference is tautological.

perturbseq.interpretation: Geodesic distance correlates with effect magnitude (rho=0.33). After matching on magnitude, essential vs non-essential difference vanishes entirely.

lincs.interpretation: Direction instability rankings are robust to feature-subset corrections. Magnitude confound does not apply here because the metric is cosine-based (normalized).
lincs.dag_arrow: No magnitude confound — metric is inherently normalized.

causal_dag.confound_structure: Effect magnitude is a common cause of (1) essentiality classification and (2) divergence metric inflation. Adjusting for magnitude removes the spurious association.

confounder_vs_mediator.depmap: Confounder — essentiality IS DEFINED BY magnitude (CERES score). Tautological.
confounder_vs_mediator.perturbseq: Confounder via estimation — larger effects produce better-estimated PCA subspaces, shifting geodesic distances regardless of true directional diversity.
confounder_vs_mediator.lincs: Not applicable — cosine-based direction instability is already magnitude-normalized.

causal_dag.fix: Normalize divergence metrics by response magnitude before comparing groups defined by effect size. Use CV (SD/|mean|), magnitude-matching, or residualization.

actionable_rule: If you compute divergence/instability metrics on perturbation data and then compare groups defined by effect strength (essential vs non-essential, toxic vs non-toxic), you MUST normalize for effect magnitude or you will mistake magnitude for divergence.
